title: Fix capture_localized_surfaces final-move + manifest write ordering
kind: backfilled-record (work shipped 2026-05-18T10:41:43Z as part of app-localization-hig-multisurface-screenshot-capture commit 028a511; this slice surfaces the scope deviation as a named slice for trail clarity after the user asked for it).
summary: automation/smoke/capture_localized_surfaces.py.capture_all placed the final shutil.move from the temp staging dir into output_dir plus the README+manifest writes AFTER the `with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` block exited. Python's tempdir teardown deleted the staged screenshots before they could be moved, so --capture failed with FileNotFoundError. Fix: indent the final-move + write_readme + write_manifest block inside the `with` block so the staging dir is still alive during the move.
files_changed: automation/smoke/capture_localized_surfaces.py (block indentation only; lines 423-431 moved inside the with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() block at line 372)
shipped_in_commit: 028a511 Capture today-surface HIG screenshots × 18 non-English locales

validation.make automation-check: passed (71 tests, 14 harness tests still green after the fix)
validation.harness self-test (actual --capture run): 18/18 captures landed in the proof directory after the fix

not_claimed: any change to harness public API or contract
any change to harness behavior beyond making --capture actually persist files
residual_risk: The fix relies on the existing harness test set (which mocks runner with a callable that never produces real files). The bug was not caught by the unit tests because they verify orchestration shape, not end-to-end file persistence. Future work could add an integration test that uses a faked runner to write minimal PNG bytes and verify files land in the output dir; that's out of scope for this backfill record.
context: User asked to surface the scope deviation noted in the screenshot-capture slice handoff as its own queue entry. The fix itself was already shipped; this slice + handoff exist for audit/trail clarity, not to redo the work.
